name: Anesthesiology
focus: Administration of anesthesia and perioperative care for surgical and procedural patients.
description: Anesthesiologists manage anesthesia, pain control, and life-support functions before, during, and after surgery.

name: Cardiology
focus: Diagnosis and treatment of heart and cardiovascular system diseases.
description: Cardiologists manage conditions affecting the heart and blood vessels, from prevention to intervention.

name: Colorectal Surgery
focus: Surgical treatment of diseases of the colon, rectum, and anus.
description: Colorectal surgeons specialize in the surgical management of disorders of the large intestine, rectum, and anus.

name: Dermatology
focus: Diagnosis and treatment of diseases affecting the skin, hair, and nails.
description: Dermatologists diagnose and treat over 3,000 conditions affecting the skin, hair, and nails, including skin cancers.

name: Emergency Medicine
focus: Immediate evaluation and treatment of acute and life-threatening conditions.
description: Emergency physicians provide rapid assessment and stabilization of patients with acute, urgent, or life-threatening conditions.

name: Endocrinology
focus: Diagnosis and management of hormonal and metabolic disorders.
description: Endocrinologists manage disorders of the endocrine glands, including the thyroid, adrenal glands, pancreas, and pituitary gland.

name: Family Medicine
focus: Comprehensive primary care for patients of all ages across all organ systems.
description: Family physicians provide continuing and comprehensive health care for individuals and families across all ages, genders, and diseases.

name: Gastroenterology
focus: Diagnosis and treatment of disorders of the gastrointestinal tract and liver.
description: Gastroenterologists specialize in the digestive system, including the esophagus, stomach, intestines, pancreas, liver, and gallbladder.

name: General Practice
focus: First-contact and continuing care for undifferentiated health problems across all ages.
description: General practitioners serve as the first point of contact in the healthcare system, managing a broad range of acute and chronic conditions.

name: General Surgery
focus: Surgical management of abdominal organs, skin, breast, and soft tissue conditions.
description: General surgeons perform a wide range of operations involving the abdomen, digestive tract, breast, skin, and soft tissues.

name: Genetics
focus: Diagnosis and management of inherited genetic conditions and chromosomal disorders.
description: Clinical geneticists diagnose and manage inherited disorders, and provide genetic counseling to patients and families at risk.

name: Geriatrics
focus: Comprehensive care for elderly patients, addressing multiple chronic conditions and functional decline.
description: Geriatricians specialize in the health care of older adults, focusing on the prevention and treatment of diseases common in old age.

name: Hematology
focus: Diagnosis and treatment of blood disorders and diseases of the bone marrow.
description: Hematologists manage disorders of the blood, including cancers such as leukemia and lymphoma, anemia, and clotting disorders.

name: Infectious Disease
focus: Diagnosis and management of complex infections caused by bacteria, viruses, fungi, and parasites.
description: Infectious disease specialists diagnose and treat complex or unusual infections and advise on antimicrobial therapy and infection control.

name: Internal Medicine
focus: Diagnosis and non-surgical management of complex diseases in adults.
procedures: procedures such as thoracentesis and paracentesis
description: Internists (hospitalists and general internists) manage complex multi-system diseases in adults, often serving as diagnosticians and care coordinators.

name: Nephrology
focus: Diagnosis and treatment of kidney diseases and management of kidney replacement therapy.
description: Nephrologists manage kidney disease, hypertension related to kidney function, and renal replacement therapy including dialysis and transplantation.

name: Neurology
focus: Diagnosis and treatment of disorders of the brain, spinal cord, and peripheral nervous system.
description: Neurologists diagnose and treat a wide spectrum of nervous system disorders, from stroke and epilepsy to neurodegenerative diseases.

name: Neurosurgery
focus: Surgical treatment of disorders of the brain, spine, and peripheral nerves.
description: Neurosurgeons perform operations on the brain, spine, and peripheral nervous system to treat tumors, trauma, vascular malformations, and degenerative conditions.

name: Obstetrics and Gynecology
focus: Women's reproductive health, pregnancy, childbirth, and gynecological conditions.
description: OB/GYN physicians manage all aspects of women's reproductive health, from routine gynecological care to high-risk obstetrics and gynecologic surgery.

name: Oncology
focus: Diagnosis and treatment of cancer using systemic therapies.
description: Medical oncologists diagnose and treat cancer with chemotherapy, immunotherapy, targeted therapy, and coordinate multidisciplinary cancer care.

name: Ophthalmology
focus: Diagnosis and treatment of diseases and disorders of the eye and vision.
description: Ophthalmologists provide medical and surgical care for the eye, managing conditions ranging from refractive errors to complex retinal diseases.

name: Orthopedics
focus: Diagnosis and surgical or non-surgical treatment of musculoskeletal system disorders.
description: Orthopedic surgeons treat injuries and diseases of the bones, joints, ligaments, tendons, muscles, and spine.

name: Otolaryngology
focus: Diagnosis and treatment of diseases of the ear, nose, throat, head, and neck.
description: ENT specialists manage disorders of the ear, nose, throat, and related structures of the head and neck, including both medical and surgical treatments.

name: Palliative Care
focus: Relief of symptoms, pain, and stress from serious illness to improve quality of life.
description: Palliative care specialists focus on relieving suffering and improving quality of life for patients with serious illnesses and their families.

name: Pathology
focus: Laboratory analysis of tissues, cells, and body fluids to diagnose disease.
description: Pathologists examine specimens from biopsies, surgeries, and autopsies to diagnose diseases, guide treatment, and advance medical knowledge.

name: Pediatrics
focus: Medical care for infants, children, and adolescents from birth through young adulthood.
description: Pediatricians manage the physical, mental, and social health of children from birth to young adulthood, with emphasis on growth, development, and prevention.

name: Plastic Surgery
focus: Reconstruction of facial and body defects and aesthetic enhancement procedures.
description: Plastic surgeons restore form and function through reconstructive procedures after trauma or cancer, and perform aesthetic surgery to alter appearance.

name: Psychiatry
focus: Diagnosis, treatment, and prevention of mental, emotional, and behavioral disorders.
description: Psychiatrists diagnose and treat mental health disorders using a combination of medication, psychotherapy, and other therapeutic approaches.

name: Pulmonology
focus: Diagnosis and treatment of diseases of the respiratory tract and lungs.
description: Pulmonologists manage diseases of the lungs and respiratory system, including obstructive, restrictive, infectious, and malignant conditions.

name: Radiology
focus: Medical imaging for the diagnosis and guided treatment of disease.
description: Radiologists interpret medical images to diagnose disease and perform image-guided interventional procedures for diagnosis and treatment.

name: Rehabilitation Medicine
focus: Restoration of function and quality of life in patients with physical impairments or disabilities.
description: Physiatrists (rehabilitation medicine specialists) diagnose and treat disabilities resulting from injury or disease to restore maximum function.

name: Rheumatology
focus: Diagnosis and treatment of autoimmune and musculoskeletal diseases.
description: Rheumatologists manage inflammatory arthritis and autoimmune diseases affecting joints, muscles, bones, and internal organs.

name: Sports Medicine
focus: Prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of injuries related to sports and physical activity.
description: Sports medicine physicians treat athletic injuries and musculoskeletal conditions, and promote physical activity for health across all age groups.

name: Trauma Surgery
focus: Emergency surgical management of life-threatening injuries from trauma.
description: Trauma surgeons provide immediate surgical intervention to patients with severe, life-threatening injuries from accidents, violence, or disasters.

name: Urology
focus: Surgical and medical management of the urinary tract and male reproductive system.
description: Urologists diagnose and treat conditions of the kidney, ureter, bladder, and urethra, as well as male reproductive disorders.

name: Vascular Surgery
focus: Surgical and endovascular treatment of diseases of arteries, veins, and lymphatics.
description: Vascular surgeons treat diseases of the blood vessels outside the heart and brain, using open surgical and minimally invasive endovascular techniques.
